steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "The gaseous ___ have molar masses of 162.15 and 56.11 g/mol, respectively." The blank must be filled with "compounds" plus the phrase "quindoxin and isobutylene." The question tests whether commas are needed around that phrase.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "compounds, quindoxin and isobutylene,": Putting commas on both sides treats "quindoxin and isobutylene" as nonessential, but without it we would not know which compounds are meant — the phrase is essential to identify the noun, so no commas are needed. ✗
  • Choice B — "compounds quindoxin and isobutylene,": This has no opening comma but places a comma after "isobutylene," creating a mismatched structure that separates "have" from its subject asymmetrically. ✗
  • Choice C — "compounds, quindoxin and isobutylene": This opens with a comma but omits the closing comma, leaving the appositive improperly bracketed and the sentence unbalanced. ✗
  • Choice D — "compounds quindoxin and isobutylene": No commas on either side. "Quindoxin and isobutylene" is a restrictive appositive — it is essential to identify which compounds are meant — so no commas are correct. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
